title: erythroxylum pervillei
intro: Erythroxylum pervillei, also known as the Vanuatu coca, is a plant species native to the Pacific island of Espiritu Santo in Vanuatu. The plant contains alkaloids, similar to those found in the coca plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. Research has shown that the medicinal properties of Erythroxylum pervillei include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties, making it potentially beneficial for treating pain, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Erythroxylum pervillei are often used in traditional medicine, but more research is needed to fully understand its health benefits and potential uses.
